rationale: We must exploit our tactical momentum with precision strikes to degrade Tethysian defensive capabilities and force political hesitation.
A forward naval deployment will signal resolve, reinforce our strategic posture, and threaten secondary avenues of pressure.
Small, rapid incursions will stretch enemy response capacity and prevent consolidation along the front lines.
Given the moderate crisis level and resilient defender information campaigns, we must prioritize hardening our networks against counter-influence and espionage to maintain operational integrity.
To complement military momentum, we will amplify narratives exposing Tethysian vulnerabilities and internal divisions, leveraging realist framing to erode their international support.
With increased air activity and battlefield movements, we must detect and neutralize Tethysian intelligence assets to protect our operational plans and supply lines.
Precision sanctions on Tethysian defense-sector allies and financial enablers will deepen economic strain without triggering broad humanitarian fallout that could erode our international legitimacy.
Seizing offshore assets of key Tethysian oligarchs linked to the war effort amplifies financial pressure and signals resolve, complementing military and information operations.
Controlled weakening of our currency will boost export competitiveness and offset sanction-related revenue losses, stabilizing domestic economic support for the campaign.
A highly visible mobilization near the eastern front will signal resolve and disrupt Novaris’s operational calculus during their limited advances.
Increased air patrols will counter escalating scrambles and protect critical infrastructure from potential airstrikes.
Covert reconnaissance will gather real-time intelligence on enemy troop movements amid heightened tensions and information warfare.
Given the heightened air activity and information warfare, we must harden our networks against potential cyber intrusions that could disrupt command and control.
With Novaris under economic strain, they may increase espionage efforts to gain asymmetric advantages; we must detect and neutralize these threats.
To complement the military’s show of force, we will amplify our narrative of resilience and deterrence across domestic and international channels.
Given moderate existing sanctions and room to escalate, we impose precision economic pain on key Novaris defense-sector oligarchs to amplify their internal economic strain.
Seizing offshore assets of sanctioned Novaris officials complements our targeted sanctions by disrupting their financial enablers and signaling resolve.
Restricting dual-use technology exports to Novaris reinforces our punitive measures and hampers their military-industrial capacity without broad collateral damage.
A targeted diplomatic offensive to key neutral states will bolster international support and counter Novaris’s narrative amid low global backing for Tethys.
Utilizing discreet channels, we probe for fissures in Novaris’s position to manage escalation risks without compromising our principled stance.
Establishing domestically controlled humanitarian corridors demonstrates our commitment to civilian protection and strengthens our moral authority internationally.
